zotero.abstractNote: We introduce the Structural Causal Marginal Problem (SCMP): given a set of marginal distributions of a structural causal model (SCM) under different interventions, can we identify the causal graph or the interventional distributions? We show that the SCMP is a powerful framework that encompasses several existing causal inference problems, such as causal discovery from interventional data, transportability, and causal effect estimation. We provide a general identifiability criterion for the SCMP and show that it can be solved using a variety of methods, including constraint-based, score-based, and continuous optimization approaches. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework on synthetic and real-world data.
